pages.title: Welcome! | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Main landing page introducing Blockchain Web Services (BWS) platform and its suite of products. Provides overview of platform APIs including IPFS upload, blockchain storage, and NFT solutions, as well as marketplace solutions for badges, gaming, and ESG credits. Highlights the mission to make blockchain accessible to non-technical users.
useCases: Upload NFT images and metadata to decentralized IPFS storage
Create digital badges and credentials on blockchain
Build sports fan engagement platforms with NFT game cubes
Track and manage ESG carbon credits and environmental impacts
Store immutable data records on blockchain without Web3 wallets

pages.title: Quick Start | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Quick start tutorial demonstrating how to write data to blockchain using BWS API in a single call. Shows developers how to authenticate with API keys and use the BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution to save "Hello World!" to Matchain blockchain without managing network fees or Web3 wallets.

codeExamples.description: API endpoint to write 'Hello World!' message to Matchain blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution

useCases: Write first blockchain transaction without Web3 wallet setup
Save text messages to blockchain immutably
Test API authentication and connectivity
Store simple data records on Matchain network
Verify blockchain transaction status and results

implementationSteps.title: Get your API key
implementationSteps.description: Obtain API key from BWS platform for authentication as all requests require a valid API key

implementationSteps.title: Write Hello World to blockchain
implementationSteps.description: Make POST request to call endpoint with BWS.Blockchain.Hash solution to save message to Matchain blockchain

implementationSteps.title: Check for Status
implementationSteps.description: Fetch the blockchain job status and results to verify transaction completion

pages.title: Platform Fees | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Explains BWS platform fee structure for blockchain transactions. Details how blockchain fees compensate miners for computational work and ensure network security. Provides fees calculator and pricing examples to help developers understand costs for using BWS solutions without managing Web3 wallets directly.
useCases: Calculate blockchain transaction costs before implementation
Budget for blockchain integration projects
Compare BWS pricing with direct blockchain interaction costs
Understand fee structure for smart contract calls
Plan operational costs for blockchain applications

pages.title: API How Tos | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview page introducing API fundamentals for BWS platform. Serves as navigation hub to detailed documentation covering API endpoint configuration, authentication methods, main API methods (call and fetch), response formats, and error handling for programmatic access to BWS solutions.
useCases: Learn BWS API authentication requirements
Understand API endpoint structure and methods
Handle API responses and error codes properly
Implement call and fetch API patterns
Integrate BWS solutions programmatically

pages.title: Certificate Of Trust | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents the Certificate of Trust feature provided by all BWS solutions. Explains how blockchain-verified certificates demonstrate transaction authenticity, trust, and transparency to customers. Certificates are automatically generated upon operation completion with shareable transaction information and proof of registry.
useCases: Provide customers with blockchain-verified transaction proof
Demonstrate data authenticity and transparency
Share verifiable certificates for compliance purposes
Build trust with blockchain-backed documentation
Create audit trails with shareable certificates

pages.title: Media Assets | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides access to BWS brand assets including logos, visual identity guidelines, and media resources. Contains downloadable BWS Brand Guideline PDF with specifications for proper use of brand elements, logos, and visual design standards for partners and developers.
useCases: Download BWS logos for partner integrations
Follow brand guidelines for co-marketing materials
Access visual assets for presentations and documentation
Ensure consistent brand representation in applications
Obtain official BWS branding for promotional use

pages.title: BWS.IPFS.Upload | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Main documentation page for BWS.IPFS.Upload solution enabling easy upload of images, PDF, and JSON files to IPFS distributed storage network. Provides overview of IPFS functionality and available operations for decentralized file storage without managing IPFS infrastructure directly.
useCases: Upload NFT images to decentralized IPFS storage
Store PDF documents on distributed network
Save JSON metadata files for blockchain assets
Create permanent decentralized file storage
Host media files without centralized servers

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Details BWS IPFS API service leveraging InterPlanetary File System for distributed, peer-to-peer storage. Explains how IPFS creates permanent decentralized data storage using content identifiers (CID), file chunking, and distributed network. Documents BWS IPFS Gateway (ipfs.bws.ninja) for accessing uploaded files via traditional web browsers.
useCases: Access IPFS files through web gateway without IPFS client
Store files with permanent content-addressed identifiers
Distribute large files across decentralized network
Create censorship-resistant file storage
Retrieve IPFS content via standard HTTPS URLs

pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference documentation for BWS.IPFS.Upload operations including upload to IPFS and list files endpoints. Details request/response formats, parameters, and methods to upload content and retrieve IPFS Content Identifiers (CID) with accessible URIs through BWS gateway.

codeExamples.description: Upload files to IPFS and receive Content Identifier (CID) with IPFS URIs for accessing the uploaded content
Response from IPFS upload operation containing the Content Identifier (CID) and accessible URIs including IPFS protocol and HTTPS gateway URL

useCases: Upload NFT artwork and receive IPFS CID
Store document files on IPFS programmatically
Generate permanent IPFS links for media content
List previously uploaded IPFS files
Integrate IPFS storage into applications

implementationSteps.title: Upload to IPFS
implementationSteps.description: Make POST request to call endpoint with solution 'BWS.IPFS.Upload' and operation 'new' to upload content and receive IPFS CID

implementationSteps.title: List IPFS Files
implementationSteps.description: Use list operation to retrieve previously uploaded IPFS files and their content identifiers

pages.title: BWS.Blockchain.Save | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Main documentation page for BWS.Blockchain.Save solution providing efficient immutable data storage on blockchain. Introduces the solution as a platform API for storing permanent, tamper-proof records on blockchain networks without requiring Web3 wallet management or blockchain expertise.
useCases: Store immutable records of important documents
Create permanent audit trails on blockchain
Save timestamped data that cannot be altered
Archive critical information with blockchain proof
Build tamper-proof data storage systems

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.Blockchain.Save provides an efficient way to store immutable data on the blockchain without technical complexity. It abstracts blockchain infrastructure, allowing users to write data and receive a Certificate of Trust for verifiable proof of integrity and authenticity.
useCases: Store verifiable records with Certificate of Trust
Save immutable data to blockchain without technical overhead
Create authenticated proof of data integrity
Enterprise blockchain data storage

pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page documents the save operation for BWS.Blockchain.Save, allowing users to write data to the blockchain. The operation is asynchronous and returns a job ID for tracking the blockchain transaction status using the fetch API.

codeExamples.description: Response from save operation containing job ID for tracking the blockchain transaction
API call to save data to blockchain with solution parameters

useCases: Save immutable records to blockchain
Create verifiable timestamps for documents
Store compliance data with blockchain proof
Archive critical business data on blockchain

implementationSteps.title: Make save API call
implementationSteps.description: POST to /v1/call endpoint with solution set to BWS.Blockchain.Save, operation as 'save', and your data in the value parameter

implementationSteps.title: Receive job ID
implementationSteps.description: API returns a job ID for tracking the asynchronous blockchain transaction

implementationSteps.title: Check transaction status
implementationSteps.description: Use fetch API with the provided job ID to check blockchain transaction status and results

pages.title: BWS.Blockchain.Hash | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.Blockchain.Hash is a navigation page that introduces using the blockchain as a hash table database. It provides links to the solution overview and operations documentation for this mutable blockchain storage service.
useCases: Use blockchain as hash table database
Store and retrieve mutable blockchain data
Implement blockchain-based key-value storage
Create decentralized database applications

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.Blockchain.Hash allows using blockchain as a hash database for efficient data storage and retrieval. A hash database uses unique keys for quick lookups, making it ideal for applications requiring rapid data access like client data management.
useCases: Save client data to blockchain with unique identifiers
Implement rapid data access with hash keys
Store business records with efficient retrieval
Create scalable blockchain database applications
Manage customer information on blockchain

pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page documents insert and select operations for BWS.Blockchain.Hash. Insert saves data with a unique key to the blockchain, while select retrieves data using that key, enabling mutable blockchain database functionality.

codeExamples.description: Response from insert operation returning job ID for tracking the blockchain transaction
Insert operation to save data with a unique key to blockchain

useCases: Store customer records with unique client IDs
Implement blockchain-based key-value database
Save and retrieve mutable business data
Create distributed hash table on blockchain
Manage user profiles with blockchain storage

implementationSteps.title: Insert data with key
implementationSteps.description: POST to /v1/call with operation 'insert', providing a unique key and data value to store on blockchain

implementationSteps.title: Track insertion job
implementationSteps.description: Use the returned job ID to monitor the blockchain transaction status

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve data using select
implementationSteps.description: Use the select operation with the same key to retrieve stored data from blockchain

pages.title: BWS.NFT.zK | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.NFT.zK is a zero-knowledge NFT management solution that simplifies NFT creation and management. This navigation page provides access to documentation covering NFT operations, ownership, and attributes for managing NFTs without complexity.
useCases: Create NFTs with zero technical complexity
Manage NFT ownership and transfers
Mint NFTs on multiple blockchain networks
Implement NFT solutions without blockchain expertise

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page explains NFT ownership models in BWS.NFT.zK, where newly created NFTs are initially linked to the BWS account. Users can transfer ownership either to blockchain wallet addresses or email addresses, with BWS providing custody before transfer.
useCases: Create NFTs with BWS account custody
Transfer NFT ownership to blockchain wallets
Send NFTs to recipients via email
Manage NFT metadata and images on IPFS
Deploy NFTs across multiple blockchain networks

implementationSteps.title: Create NFT
implementationSteps.description: NFT is initially created and linked to your BWS account with BWS providing custody

implementationSteps.title: Choose transfer method
implementationSteps.description: Select to transfer ownership either to a blockchain wallet address or to an email address

implementationSteps.title: Complete ownership transfer
implementationSteps.description: Execute the transfer to move NFT ownership from BWS custody to the recipient

pages.title: Operations | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Complete API reference for NFT.zK operations including creating, listing, transferring, and sending NFTs via email. All operations use asynchronous processing with job IDs for tracking blockchain transactions across multiple networks.

codeExamples.description: Endpoint for creating (minting) a new NFT on selected blockchain network

useCases: Mint new NFTs programmatically via API
List all NFTs owned by account
Transfer NFT ownership to wallet addresses
Send NFTs to users via email
Track NFT creation with asynchronous job monitoring

implementationSteps.title: Call create NFT operation
implementationSteps.description: POST to /v1/call with operation 'create', network, and NFT parameters including name, description, and image

implementationSteps.title: Receive job ID
implementationSteps.description: API returns job ID as the operation is asynchronous due to blockchain confirmation time

implementationSteps.title: Check transaction status
implementationSteps.description: Use fetch API with job ID to monitor blockchain transaction status and get receipt

pages.title: NFT Attributes (traits) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page explains NFT attributes (traits) which are specific characteristics that make each NFT unique. It demonstrates metadata structure with examples like animated cat NFTs featuring attributes such as fur color, eye type, and accessories.

codeExamples.description: Example NFT metadata structure showing how to define traits/attributes for a digital art collection

useCases: Define unique characteristics for NFT collections
Create digital art with specific traits
Implement rarity systems with attribute combinations
Build NFT metadata for collectible items
Structure traits for gaming NFTs

pages.title: API Endpoint | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documentation page specifying the BWS API endpoint location at api.bws.ninja with current API version v1. This is the base URL for all Blockchain Web Services API calls.

codeExamples.description: Base endpoint URL for all BWS API v1 calls

useCases: Configure API client with base endpoint
Make API calls to BWS services
Access version 1 of BWS API
Set up development environment for BWS integration

pages.title: Authentication | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page explains how to authenticate API requests to the BWS platform using the X-Api-Key header attribute. All API calls must include the API key in the request headers for authorization.

codeExamples.description: JavaScript AJAX example showing how to include the API key in request headers

useCases: Authenticate API requests to BWS platform
Secure access to blockchain services
Authorize API calls for all BWS solutions

implementationSteps.title: Add API Key Header
implementationSteps.description: Include 'X-Api-Key' header attribute with your API key value in all API requests

pages.title: Get your API Key | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page provides instructions on how to obtain your API key from the BWS platform. Users need to sign in at bws.ninja and navigate to their profile to access and copy their API key.
useCases: Obtain API credentials for BWS platform access
Set up authentication for blockchain API integration
Retrieve personal API key for development

implementationSteps.title: Sign In
implementationSteps.description: Sign in to your account at bws.ninja

implementationSteps.title: Navigate to Profile
implementationSteps.description: Go to Profile from the top right menu

implementationSteps.title: Access API Key
implementationSteps.description: Select API Key option to view and copy your API key

pages.title: Main API Methods | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page introduces the two main API methods used by BWS Unified API: Call and Fetch. The Call operation initiates blockchain requests while Fetch retrieves the status of asynchronous operations due to blockchain's validation process.
useCases: Initiate asynchronous blockchain operations
Check status of pending blockchain transactions
Handle blockchain network validation delays

implementationSteps.title: Use Call Method
implementationSteps.description: Send data to blockchain and initiate the request

implementationSteps.title: Use Fetch Method
implementationSteps.description: Poll for operation status and retrieve results

pages.title: 'call' API Method | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page documents the 'call' API method endpoint at https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call. It provides a code example demonstrating how to use BWS.Blockchain.Save to write data to the Mumbai blockchain network using the save operation.

codeExamples.description: JavaScript example showing how to write 'Hello World!' to the Mumbai blockchain using BWS.Blockchain.Save

useCases: Write data to blockchain networks
Initiate blockchain save operations
Store immutable records on Mumbai testnet
Execute blockchain solution operations

implementationSteps.title: Set API Endpoint
implementationSteps.description: Use POST method to https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call

implementationSteps.title: Configure Request Body
implementationSteps.description: Include solution, version, network, operation, and parameters in JSON body

implementationSteps.title: Add Headers
implementationSteps.description: Include Content-Type and X-Api-Key headers with your API key

pages.title: 'fetch' API Method | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page documents the 'fetch' API method endpoint at https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch. It explains how to check the status of asynchronous blockchain operations using a jobId returned from previous API calls.

codeExamples.description: JavaScript AJAX example to fetch job status and results using a jobId

useCases: Check status of pending blockchain operations
Retrieve results from completed blockchain transactions
Poll for asynchronous operation completion
Monitor blockchain job progress

implementationSteps.title: Get JobId
implementationSteps.description: Obtain the jobId from the initial call API response

implementationSteps.title: Send Fetch Request
implementationSteps.description: POST to https://api.bws.ninja/v1/fetch with jobId in request body

implementationSteps.title: Check Status
implementationSteps.description: Review the response to determine operation status and retrieve results

pages.title: API Responses | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page describes the structure of API responses from BWS API, including statusCode, statusMessage, and info parameters. It explains the difference between HTTP transport errors and controlled errors related to parameter values.

codeExamples.description: Example API response structure showing statusCode, statusMessage, and info object

useCases: Parse API response data
Handle successful API calls
Extract information from API responses
Implement error detection logic

implementationSteps.title: Check Status Code
implementationSteps.description: Verify statusCode is 200 for successful operations

implementationSteps.title: Read Status Message
implementationSteps.description: Review optional statusMessage for additional context

implementationSteps.title: Extract Info
implementationSteps.description: Access the info object to retrieve requested data

pages.title: Error Status Codes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page lists all possible error status codes returned by the BWS API, ranging from 400 to 503. Each error code includes its meaning and description to help developers troubleshoot API call issues.

codeExamples.description: Example API response showing a 500 error status code

useCases: Troubleshoot API authentication issues
Handle bad request errors
Implement retry logic for server errors
Debug API parameter problems

pages.title: BWS Logo | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page provides downloadable BWS logo assets in various formats and sizes including SVG, PNG, and versions optimized for dark backgrounds. Logos are available in multiple resolutions from 32x32 to 256x256 pixels.
useCases: Display BWS branding in applications
Add BWS logo to documentation
Include BWS brand in marketing materials
Integrate BWS logo in user interfaces

pages.title: Snapshots | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page serves as a directory for BWS.IPFS.Upload related media assets and snapshots. It links to resources showing how to use the IPFS file upload service through the BWS platform.
useCases: View IPFS upload implementation examples
Access visual guides for IPFS integration
Reference IPFS upload code samples

pages.title: BWS.IPFS.Upload | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page displays a source code snapshot demonstrating how to upload files to IPFS using the BWS platform API. It provides visual reference material for developers implementing IPFS file upload functionality.
useCases: Upload files to IPFS network
Store NFT images on IPFS
Implement decentralized file storage
Upload media content to distributed storage

pages.title: BWS.Blockchain.Badges | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.Blockchain.Badges revolutionizes how organizations recognize and validate individual achievements by leveraging blockchain technology's immutable and transparent nature. The solution enables seamless issuance, management, and verification of digital credentials with blockchain certification, making every achievement recognizable and verifiable across platforms.
useCases: Issue verifiable digital credentials for educational achievements
Manage and verify professional certifications on blockchain
Create tamper-proof employee achievement badges
Recognize and validate individual accomplishments across platforms
Integrate blockchain-certified credentials into custom systems

pages.title: Badges User Interface | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: This page provides a comprehensive guide to using the Blockchain Badges user interface for issuing and managing verifiable digital credentials. It covers the no-code solution for companies to add issuers, define badges, and award certificates built on open badge standards with blockchain certification for secure, tamper-proof credentials.
useCases: Add and manage badge issuers through user interface
Define new badge types for various achievements
Award digital certificates to recipients via email
Search and track awarded credentials
Issue verifiable credentials without coding

implementationSteps.title: Manage Issuers
implementationSteps.description: Set up and configure organizations or entities that will issue badges and credentials

implementationSteps.title: Define New Badges
implementationSteps.description: Create badge templates with specific criteria and achievement requirements

implementationSteps.title: Reward Badge Awards
implementationSteps.description: Issue credentials to recipients and send award notifications via email

implementationSteps.title: Search Awards
implementationSteps.description: Track and manage issued credentials through the search interface

pages.title: BWS.ESG.Credits | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: BWS.ESG.Credits solution helps financial institutions quickly integrate ESG impact benefits into their digital channels and investment reporting by leveraging investments in green assets. It serves banks, investment advisors, institutional investors, venture capitalists, and issuers by enabling creation of detailed ESG impact reports supporting ICMA frameworks and multi-currency reporting.
useCases: Integrate ESG impact reporting into bank digital channels
Generate investment reports with environmental impact data
Publish quality-assured impact data through certification bodies
Track green asset investments for institutional investors
Provide ESG benefit reporting for venture capital portfolios

pages.title: BWS.NFT.GameCube | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: NFT Game Cube is a scalable B2B2C platform solution that provides new revenue streams for sports clubs worldwide. The solution enables fans to play multiple sports games including weekend chess tournaments, soccer VIP finals, and cricket yearly seasons, creating a new sports fan experience and engagement model.
useCases: Create new revenue streams for sports clubs
Engage fans through NFT-based sports gaming
Host weekend chess tournaments with NFT rewards
Organize soccer VIP finals with digital collectibles
Run cricket yearly season competitions

pages.title: X BOT | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: X Bot is a Telegram-based analytics platform that provides accurate X (Twitter) engagement metrics using the official X API. It helps crypto projects measure community impact and KOL profitability, enables KOL teams to showcase verifiable performance, and allows investors to discover projects with authentic community traction.
useCases: Measure crypto project community impact and engagement
Track KOL profitability and performance metrics
Showcase verifiable KOL team performance data
Discover crypto projects with authentic community traction
Analyze Twitter engagement using official X API data
